reviews.review_text:
-Beautiful art style.
-Great OST.
-Lovely story.
-Hard gameplay.

10/10

Okay this game has completely blown me away! I was never a fan of 2D Metroidvania games and this one has definitely made me change my mind and more. This game has one of the best environmental story telling I have ever seen in video games or even other forms of media. With the interconnected maps of the Kingdom of Hallownest, to the differences in architecture, to the concealed dark underground labyrinths to the characters and the [spoiler] dream spirit fights and dialogues [/spoiler] all over the Kingdom, everything is telling the story. Amazing world-building, absolutely no lore dumps or expositions but for someone who reads through the journals and observes the dialogues (trained by multiple Fromsoft games) I had an amazing time in the world of Hollow Knight. 

The gameplay and movement was really amazing and crisp, there are some hard platforming and traversal sections but the controls were really tight making it feel good instead of it being a chore. There are no dead end regions, there is no way to get stuck or soft-locked, they really thought everything through when they designed the world. And the music, my God, it was perfect! The area OSTs were amazing, I need to hear the boss OSTs again because I was too busy getting my ass kicked. This is definitely a souls-like game with how difficult and simply tricky the bosses and mini-bosses were. No complicated movesets but boy do they kick ass. 

After almost 32 hours in game I was able to finish the basic ending with a 87% completion. Cannot wait to get back for the DLCs and other optional stuff left. A definite 10/10, buy it, there's really no equivalent to this game in this genre!

Update after 52 hours: I returned after the basic ending and went through much of the optional and DLC content, all the way to the true ending route and man was it worth every second!  The way the game slowly reveals the history of Hallownest, [spoiler] the nature of the Vessels, the Pale King, the Abyss, and the source of the infection [/spoiler] is truly remarkable. The optional bosses and late game challenges are brutal, making me even more scared of the Riddler iykwim. I am stopping at 100% for now, which is short of full 112%, not because I am tired of the game, but because I want to leave with the magic intact and return later. This is easily one of the greatest games I have played.

(I rank with a 5 star system)

Overall Impression

Hollow knight is a 2D Metroidvania that I didn’t think much of in the year 2026 considering where gaming is today. I was completely wrong. This game I felt was so simple has become one of my all-time favorite games.

Gameplay ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

Hollow Knight is very expansive game. Large map and a plethora of abilities to unlock. It also doesn’t hold your hand much so you’re left to discover and roam around the map. At times I was lost and I learned to make use of my map and mark things or points to come back to. I also love the combat. I am biased as souls-like is my favorite genre and this game brings a lot of it. From enemies to bosses I found my share of difficulty.

Story ★ ★ ★ 

I feel as though most souls-like lack a direct story most times and are somewhat ambiguous. Though I get the general story the lore is much more rich as to why everything is happening. I watched a YouTube vid that gave me more of an understanding and much more appreciation and intrigue to the story of Hallownest. Unfortunately without the lore hunters I don’t feel as though I’d understand as much of what’s going on just from my own playthrough.

Graphics/Visual Style/Audio ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

Though this is a 2D game I didn’t think I could appreciate as much as I did the art. Each biome felt like a completely different land and all of the interactions and art all fit within the theme of each biome. Very immersive. I would say the audio was decent and not too much to write home (I’m not really an audiophile) about other than it was good and matched most of the biomes and bosses that I’d fight.

Performance ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

Game is optimized well with no bugs to mention in my playthrough. I played the whole game using a controller. Very intuitive controls. Good UI that’s easy to navigate. 

Length/Replayability/Value ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

The game is decently long as I got lost and there’s some difficulty while learning bosses. It took me around 20hrs or so to beat the first time. There’s a lot of replayability if you’re an achievement hunter like me. And especially the Godhome expansion where it challenges you more and more through an intense gauntlet. Insane value for the price considering I have over 100hrs.

Pros/Cons

I feel as though the pros and cons can be on the same list for many but for me personally I enjoy the combat, exploration, immersion, biomes and map, and rich lore.

Final Verdict ★ ★ ★ ★ ★

One of my all-time favorite games and definite recommend!

After just climbing to the summit of Pantheon Five, all I have to say is that this game is one that will be remembered as an all time classic. Stunning visuals, great and enjoyable gameplay, an interesting lore and story, amazing music: it's all you'd want. I haven't played any metroidvanias before, but this is a huge recommendation!

Hollow Knight is a side-scrolling adventure game built around exploration and movement through connected areas. The visual style is hand-drawn and detailed, with a dark tone that gives each region a distinct identity. 

There is very little direct instruction on where to go, so progress mainly comes from paying attention to surroundings and slowly learning how different paths connect. At first it can feel unclear, but over time the layout starts to make more sense as new routes open after gaining movement abilities.

Movement between areas often requires returning to earlier places once new skills are obtained, which changes how older sections are viewed. Each region has its own map structure that has to be uncovered step by step, and locating mapping points becomes important for keeping track of routes. 

The combat is based on timing and positioning rather than simple attacks, with enemies following different patterns that require adjustment in approach. It is a highly engaging experience that rewards patience and exploration, and is a strong choice for those who like interconnected world design and skill-based progression.

I played the game using keyboard and mouse despite having an Xbox controller, because I personally prefer keyboard and mouse controls, even though I assume most players would choose a controller. To check and set up the keyboard and mouse binds, a screenshot of the layout is attached below as a reference for keyboard and mouse players.

Overall, the game offers a large world with many hidden routes, optional paths, and multiple possible outcomes based on how much is discovered. The control system works well and allows precise movement during both platform sections and battles. It stands out as a well-crafted experience with attention to detail and steady exploration throughout the entire journey from start to finish.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3733128589

absolutely breathtaking game. perhaps the best i have ever played. 

upsides:
- immersion is next level. perhaps the most entranced i have felt by a fictional world. getting lost in the environment of hollownest was an experience like no other
- incredible sound design, ost, artstyle. i love its moody gothic aesthetic contrasting with the cute npcs
- lore is amazing. best environmental storytelling i have seen in a game.
- combat is challenging, but rewarding and satisfying

downsides:
- takes a while to get into it: presevere, don't ragequit for two years and then only return to it later like i did. i had no idea what i was missing out on.
- combat can be punishing at times.
- lore can be obscure and hard to piece together. but that's easily fixed by watching mossbag's lore video on youtube (go watch it it's a banger)
- lots of bugs (get it?)

conclusion: it's more than just video game, it's an experience. the hype is real. play hollow knight. if you like any sort of non-linear, combat-heavy, exploration-heavy video games, or just indie games that are obviously crafted with nothing but pure love and unrivaled talent, play this game. seriously. you won't regret it.

Hollow Knight is a masterpiece in terms of metroidvanias and platformers, and honestly one of the true GOATs of the industry.

The game starts simple, but steadily and naturally increases the difficulty without ever feeling unfair. Every new area, enemy, and boss feels like a proper evolution of everything you learned before. It constantly pushes you to improve while still making progression feel satisfying.

What makes the game truly special is how alive the world feels. Hallownest is filled with amazing lore, memorable biomes, hidden secrets, and so many traversal methods that exploring never becomes boring. Unlocking new movement abilities completely changes how you view older areas, and even moving from one place to another stays fun throughout the entire game.

The combat is another huge strength. It feels fast, responsive, and satisfying in a way very few games truly master. Boss fights especially are incredible because they test skill and patience instead of relying on cheap mechanics. Every victory feels earned.

The atmosphere, soundtrack, art style, and environmental storytelling all come together perfectly to create a world that feels mysterious, tragic, and unforgettable at the same time.

All in all, Hollow Knight is not just one of the best indie games ever made, but genuinely one of the greatest games of all time.

I cannot recommend this more highly to anyone comfortable with platforming or who enjoys metroidvanias. I think the majority of people will enjoy the aesthetics, the smooth animations, the challenging enemy patterns, and the rewarding upgrades enough to be able to appreciate the journey and to try until they succeed.

However, I cannot recommend the game to the average person. While I enjoyed the parts I played, I struggled (as a casual) to discover where to go, and I definitely struggled against some of the bosses. I have not completed the game and I did enjoy a lot of what I did play. Definitely a skill issue on my part, but it does not change the fact that i would only recommend to people already fans of the genre. Still a very lovely game.

Hollow Knight is one of those rare games that completely pulled me in from start to finish. At first, I thought it was just another indie platformer, but the deeper I explored Hallownest, the more I realized how much care and passion went into every part of the game. The world feels alive despite being a fallen kingdom, with beautiful environments, memorable characters, and an atmosphere that constantly makes you want to see what's around the next corner.

The exploration is incredibly rewarding because the game doesn't hold your hand. Every new area feels like a genuine discovery, and finding hidden paths or secrets always gave me a sense of accomplishment. The combat is simple but satisfying, and the boss fights are some of the best I've experienced in a Metroidvania game. Many of them took me multiple attempts, but defeating them never felt unfair—it felt earned.

The soundtrack deserves special praise as well. It perfectly matches the mood of each area and made many moments even more memorable. While the game can be challenging and sometimes leaves you feeling lost, that's also part of what makes the adventure so special.

If you enjoy exploration, challenging combat, and games that trust players to figure things out on their own, Hollow Knight is an easy recommendation. It's easily one of the best indie games I've ever played and absolutely worth every hour I spent in it.

It's undoubtedly cool, and I wanted to give it a try, but it's not really for me.  I put down Ori and the Blind Forest because its whimsical fantasy theme wasn't compelling to me, and Hollow Knight didn't change my mind on that, even though it has a high level of quality and passion.  A nitpick would be the mechanic of buying limited numbers and types of map pins, which I found to be restrictive rather than fun.

I recommend Hollow Knight for fans of Metroidvanias, or if you like what it's going for artistically.

"To witness secrets sealed, one must endure the harshest punishment."
Does 112% the game multiple times, including Path of Pain, for fun count?
Maybe the wait for Silksong does.

This isn't just a game, it's a masterpiece.
"No cost too great. No mind to think. No will to break."
One of the best games I've ever played.

Patience and curiosity are highly valued and rewarded in this game. If you possess neither of those traits, worry not, the game will instill them into you, one way or another, while you enjoy the soundtracks, story and battles.

This game is just magestic i dont the best computer but still with 20 fps i managed to finish and witness this peak game. The story is amazing, the music is one of the best i ahve head ever. If you are thinking of getting this game just dont think twice, get it.

Not worth 15 dollars, it worth WAY more. The art style, the combat, the movement- it's all so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ good. As of writing this review I just unlocked the true ending to this game and it's one of two games to make me shed a tear while watching the end credits roll. Not just because of the story, because of what I've overcome from the game and mentally. There were times where I wanted to quit because of a boss I got stuck on or a certain place where I kept on dying (iykyk). But I kept on playing because it kept on hooking me back in for more and more. There isn't much I can say without spoiling certain boss fights or areas in the game so I will leave it at that.

Please play this game, you won't regret it. :]

(unless you don't like bugs and have severe Arachnophobia, if that's the case don't play it LOL)

Amazing game with great athmospere, music and visuals
Was fun but with goosebumps and day'n'night playing

I just can't express how great of a game Hollow Knight is. From simple gameplay mechanics to a beautiful soundtrack and amazing art direction, Hollow Knight is by far one of the best video games I have ever played. 

The story is epic, tragic and beautiful. Even though the lore isn't explicitly told through the game, the mystery adds to the depth of the story as well. Alongside lore, there is huge diversity of characters, both enemies and NPCs that make the world of Hollow Knight alive. Once you enter Greenpath, you will begin appreciating the art direction. The designs and artistic choices of the locations also is involved in the story telling which is very cool. The soundtrack is a masterpiece on its own, and I think you will start to realize it once you get into City of Tears. 

I would like to write why do I love this game so much but it would be a very long review, so to sum all this up, Hollow Knight excels in so many ways. It is not everyone's cup of tea, but if you are interested in this game in any way, I recommend you giving a try. And take your time playing this game, don't rush it. The first-time experience is something different.

10/10 game in my opinion. Try Hollow Knight: Silksong as well if you like this game.
im just so mind blown that a game could make me love playing games again. I havent played a game so much since my childhood and here i am 180 hours later. Just amazing
One of my favorite games of all time! Beautiful scenery and incredible music. Going for 100% can be challenging but very satisfying to finally acheive. Go on, play it!

The 100% grind was justifiable and the game challenged me real hard on the Pantheons. Speedruns and steel soul was completly fine and doable.

Overall incredible game with wonderful story and gameplay.

10/10 game. This is an absolute masterpiece, with beautiful design, nice bosses and really cool game mechanics. It demands perseverance, but it brings a lot of satisfaction once you finally win. Definitely one of my favorite games

I realised just how much I love this game only when I booted it up half a year after finishing it. Hearing the Dirtmouth music and just jumping on basic platforms brought back so many wonderful memories. The game as a single piece has such a strong personality, it provokes wonder, isolation, loneliness, triumph and all of them are perfectly tied to the moment-to-moment gameplay i.e. there are no moments when the game just tells you to feel a certain way through a cutscene or something. I don't want to dissect the game and analyse it in parts because I believe it is a strong and cohesive whole. Whichever element of the game you name, I'll likely love it.

But I will name some of the drawbacks, in my opinion. 
Difficulty-wise, standard traversal and combat felt too easy compared to many of the boss encounters (probably the soulsiest aspect of the game).
The endings, well, the fact that there are multiple significantly different ones already raises alarms. My biggest gripe is that one of these endings is clearly the one true™ ending when looking at the amount of effort required to achieve it and context of the story. They're not on equal footing. And no matter which ending you get, it feels a bit too abrupt. It needed a good epilogue, really.

That's about it, though! The fact that there's so little to criticise speaks a lot about the game. No matter what kind of games you're usually into, I strongly recommend you play Hollow Knight.

9/10

Huge respect and thanks to Team Cherry for creating such a masterpiece. Everything in this game is incredible: the atmosphere, music, exploration, bosses, lore, and gameplay.
This game gave me an experience I will never forget. I know for sure that I’m going to replay it many times because of how much I loved it.
One day, I’m even going to make my future kids play this game so they can experience this masterpiece too.
Thank you, Team Cherry, for making one of the greatest games ever created.

It was a decently fun experience, at least enough to play through it. I don't like how there's no difficulty setting though, I would've preferred an easier experience. Towards the end, I just installed some mods to make the game more fun to play. Of course, I know some people play this exactly because they like the challenge, but I only enjoy a moderate challenge as it's a way for me to relax and I don't find it fun to run back all the time to re-do the same fight dozens of times, not enough energy for that during my spare time.

Anyway, yeah, a pretty nice game overall. Going to play silksong now.
Great game and for the fraction of price of AAA games but better than many of them

single player only!

I've played the first 4 Metroid 2D titles before, and I find that Hollow Knight is more difficult than them in some of it ways. I really enjoyed the atmosphere that game built. Although most of the bosses (even mini-bosses) are difficult, I kind of enjoy fighting them, except for a few. And for the maps and obstacles, it require intermediate to expert "Metroidvania" proficiency. I really enjoyed the areas and the "platforming" aspect of this game.

My main problem would be the unclear linear-progression backbone of this game. While the environment shows one thing or two about you should explore it later (backtrack), sometimes I kind of lost and overwhelmed of what should I do next.  Compared to Metroid games that have comprehensive environment for backtracking and all in my opinion.

Basically, there's too much content being put on non-linearly. I guess that is why most of casual players quit the game. But overall, it's an impressive game and I really like it.
Great Combat, Adaptive Difficulty, Cool Bosses and Path of Pain.
This game offers lots of boss fights, an amazing story, music, and fun easter eggs. However you are at risk at ragequitting as it is a very hard game.

Ahem... 

When I first started playing Hollow Knight, I was very bad at it.

Very. Bad.

However, this did not deter me, and little by little I continued onward throughout the game, slowly getting better at the platforming, at the combat, the fact that it was  metrovania, a game genre I didn't know existed, I had just heard good things about it and wanted to try something I'd never tried before.

While traveling through the world, I discovered a bug being eaten by an enormous, flying creature. I knew I had to save him, so after far too many tries, I saved him... I saved Zote. I expected gratitude, a thank you, or perhaps some cocky wanna be hero telling me he had it under control... I got the last one, but it was... ruder than I ever could have imagined... insulting, degrading, just a horrible, horrible bug... yet, I was fond of this horrible little creature. "Perhaps I'll encounter him again, and I'll be able to help him change into a better bug on my journey!" I thought to myself, and happily continued on my way, happy to have saved a life.

Some time later, and after much more improvement, I found a hidden passage way in the Fungal wastes, and within it, at the top of what at the time was a very difficult platforming challenge for me, I saw a lost, scared, bug, clearly on the verge of being taken by the infection. I kept trying, and trying, and trying to traverse this not even difficult challenge, determined to save this bug, whoever they were, and return them to safety, and eventually, I did... I saved Bretta.

Instantly I fell in love with Bretta (not romantically of course), she was so sweet, so shy, so lost, feeling completely and utterly forgotten, yet here I was, and I was now her only friend.

Continuing on with my travels, I would frequently stop into Dirtmouth, and would always check on Bretta, for I didn't want her to feel alone, nor forgotten. Her infatuation with Ghost was honestly endearing to me, for she was never weird about it, she was never obsessive, nor odd, she was just shy, sweet, and quiet, even when her diary entries offered a completely different side of her. Even if her stories were exaggerated, even if they weren't real, I always looked forward to reading the next entry, for it was nice seeing someone so real within that small town. Dare I think it, I wanted to be the white knight she saw me as. Visiting Bretta became a wonderful tradition for me, and sitting beside her on the bench gave me a huge sense of connection to the world, a reason to always come back to Dirtmouth.

Then I went into Deepnest.

There, I once again discovered Zote in peril, and once again I rushed in to save him, hoping that perhaps this time he'd show a bit of character growth, or even a half baked attempt at a thank you. I did not receive this, I instead received insults, degradation, and rudeness. Still though, I just rolled my eyes, and thought to myself, "Oh Zote, when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on, happy to have saved a life again.

Much later, once I was in the Colosseum of Fools, I once again found Zote, though this time locked in a cage, with once again nothing but insults to offer me. I laughed to myself, wished him luck, and entered the Challenge of the Warrior. I was mortified when at the end a huge, shaking cage dropped from above, only to burst into laughter when Zote appeared, waving his wooden sword pathetically and tripping on his own feet. Reluctantly, for I was worried I'd kill him, I did fight him, and sent him flying, sticking him in the ground by his own horns.

"Oh Zote," I thought to myself, "when will you learn?" Thus, I continued on with my journeys, wondering when Zote would change...

Then I got back to Dirtmouth.

I expected to see Bretta sitting on the bench, or perhaps sleeping in her room, a new diary entry ready to be read, a new story about her white knight just waiting to be seen. I was so looking forward to seeing her again, it truly had been a long time, and I looked forward to our silent time together...

What did I find?

ZOTE.

I found ZOTE, rambling on so loudly that the whole town could hear it, with Bretta... My dear, sweet, shy Bretta, blushing, listening to his monstrous, horrible, inflated lies, completely and utterly enamored.

My world went black.

Instantly I rushed into her room, only to find it had been overtaken by ZOTE. ZOTE not filled her journal, while any mentions of me were dashed to pieces by this horrible creatures lies, framing me as a cowardly villain within his own story. Images of ZOTE now filled her walls, the plushy of Ghost now replaced with something far more grotesque, more annoying, more... ZOTE.

Just when all hope seemed lost, I then found it... a basement within Bretta's home... which held a great, paper mache stature of ZOTE with dream particles coming off of it.

This was it, if I could beat this, I could rid myself of ZOTE.

I quickly entered the battle, needle drawn and ready, prepared to face on whatever horrors awaited me...

Or so I thought...

I wasn't expecting to see Bretta atop a bed, being fanned and served by ZOTE'S... nor did I expect the GREY PRINCE ZOTE.

I'll not go into detail, I simply cannot describe it, truly, it was... grotesque.

However, in the end I vanquished the enemy, and upon emerging victorious, I quickly ran back to find Bretta, ready to hear her tell off Zote, to kick him out of the town, to find herself and her confidence!

Instead, she now looked rather uninfatuated, bored even, with her once Grey Prince. Liking what I saw, but confused all the same, I returned to the basement, only to find that the dream particles were still there. Knowing what I had to do, I returned into that wretched abyss, ready to fight him however many times I needed to to win her back. I battled against ZOTE four times, each battle becoming more and more unfathomable, truly, grotesque in every way imaginable.

When I earned my fourth victory, I emerged, hoping to find my Bretta, hoping to discover ZOTE gone, or being told off, something, anything!

What I found instead shattered me to my very core.

I found Bretta gone, and Zote still rambling on loudly to no one at all.

Panicked, I searched her diary for answers, and to my horror discovered that she had indeed awakened from ZOTE'S spell... and left Hollownest entirely because of it in order to truly find herself.

Gone.

My dear, sweet, shy Bretta was gone...

NOW REPLACED WITH ZOTE!!!!!!!!

I did everything I could to make him leave, I listened to all his precepts, I fought his dream form until I simply couldn't do it anymore, I did EVERYTHING!!!

But he just wouldn't leave.

I was stuck with him, rambling for the whole town to hear, for the rest of my playthrough...

While Bretta was gone forever.

I was heartbroken.

...

I've since started my second playthrough, and once again found myself staring up at ZOTE, being chewed on by that great, wonderful vengefly. I could have saved him, I'm better now, it wouldn't have been a problem, I'm a great player. 

Why on earth would I do that though, when I can keep Bretta safe in Dirtmouth, and perhaps more importantly, safe from ZOTE? When I can keep Dirtmouth safe from ZOTE?

Ladies and gentlemen, non-binary friends, do you know why you should play Hollow Knight? The real reason you should play Hollow Knight?

It's so that you can get the NEGLECT achievement, and truly, truly, keep Dirtmouth, and Hollownest for that matter as well as all it's inhabitants safe from that horrible, rude, insulting, lying, terrible, monstrous, ugly, belittling, manipulating, stupid, rambling, annoying, insidious bug known as ZOTE.

Thank you for your time.

i would not be considered the best gamer, and this game is VERY challenging for me. but I adore the art style and ambience. I would recommend this to anyone who likes a challenge and cutey patootie little bug guys <3
A simple review cannot truly reflect such a glorious game

Perfect game , Challenging like a souls puzzles , secrets , game does not hold your hand but once you understand how to create builds for your combat style and learn what works against certain bosses the game becomes less a struggle for your life and more of a struggle for theirs. remember your not in there with them theyre in there with you.

I've struggled a lot with this game, but that's part of what makes it fun. I'm still fairly early on, but I love the animations and all the characters. The game has a very unique personality. Once I'm done with this game, I'm definitely gonna play Silksong. Definitely worth the small price. There is no way they included both DLCs for free. Keep up the good work Team Cherry!

I played Silksong before I played this game, so I will try to not compare the two, as both are extraordinarily unique and offer different experiences for many players, including myself.
After beating Silksong, I moved on to this game. Looking back, I wish I had beaten this game before playing Silksong, since I didn't get much of the references in [spoiler]the Red Memory[/spoiler] but I did have some cool moments where I saw pieces of the story lining up. Other than that, this game is extremely well made. For a metroidvania, I kind of like the more linear gameplay (in my opinion...) and raw story that this game offers. Hollow Knight has well balanced and expertly themed areas, enemies, and songs that provide an epic, serene quality and ambiance that this game deserves. Every single one of the bosses are amazing (besides Grey Defenders), but I'm not sweaty enough to do the pantheons.
I think the only thing slightly odd is that [spoiler]Dung Defender[/spoiler] has a different art style than everything else. It just feels the tiny bit out of place, but at the same time, it makes the game even more classic. There are some parts that feel a little repetitive and dull, and platforms that feel more like a maze than parkour, but that shouldn't stop you at all from enjoying yourself.
This game is phenomenal.
I recommend.

Spectacular game

I'd suggest not grinding to hell because if you do you can literally face tank the first 4 pantheons without even the second last dmg upgrade. The 3rd last boss of the 5th is the first boss you can't face tank.

Overall peak game and definitely fair, great story imo, addictive movement.

Great game, a bit hard at first. I recommend using mods with Lumafly to remove the Shade on your first playthrough, earlygame is pretty hard first time otherwise. Also, always buy maps and explorers compass.

But THIS GAME BROUGHT BACK MY PASSION FOR GAMES.

10/10 
I regret not playing it sooner.

Why did it take me so long to play this game? I don't know. Do I regret not playing it sooner? Absolutely. 

I you like metroidvanias then you will like this one. It quite literally is a flagship game for the genre alongside metriod.

Pros: Movement is fantastic. The music and atmosphere is on point. The art needs to be in a museum. The build options that can be done with the charms is incredible. The in game maps are done very well and the map markers in this edition are well done as well. The atmospheric storytelling that is done is also fantastic. I'm just waiting for a good enough sale to buy Silksong now!

Cons: Essentially zero direction for what to do and where you need to go. I spent several hours on google and Reddit trying to understand what I needed to do to get the 118% completion. Also, some of the later game bosses were much more difficult than the final fight (If not doing true ending) And some of the pantheon fights and radiance fights are incredibly unfair and almost impossible to beat. (Granted they are 100% optional and supposed to be a challenge but for some of them, not getting hit is essentially impossible unlike path of pain which is just really difficult).

Hollow Knight is a masterpiece of the Metroidvania genre. Its combination of exploration, challenging gameplay, beautiful presentation, and deep world-building makes it an unforgettable experience. While its difficulty and lack of direction may frustrate some players, those willing to invest time into the game will find one of the most rewarding adventures available. it is a must-play for fans who enjoy exploration, platforming, and challenging action games.
This indie masterpiece is so good for its price and age. Its better than most triple A slop. Highly recommended.
Simplesmente um dos melhores jogos que já joguei na vida, eu recomendo qualquer ser humano jogá-lo, apenas compre um dos melhores jogos já feitos pela humanidade
Genuinely one of the best games I have ever played, it's difficult, but EXTREMELY rewarding with the sense of accomplishment it gives.

I have not died so much in any recent game that I have played more than this one. It was fun to try different strategies and eventually winning battles. 

The lore is a bit confusing most of the game, causing me have a trillion theories. A lot of the lore is hidden behind upgraded bosses and optional game content which is not really known until you "just keep" playing.  This is a little unfortunate but it is what it is.

Overall, I still recommend.
This is a great indie game and is incredibly well made. Thanks team cherry for an awesome game.

No gore.
Challenging platforming.
Challenging boss fights.
Many bosses.
tons of optional bosses.
Multiple endings.
Speed-runnable.
Reasonable difficulty curve.
Awesome mods available that can make the game more fun, longer, or alter the difficulty. Mods to play multiplayer (but I haven't actually done that)
Cons:
Sometime the trek back to a boss is treacherous. After you've fought a boss a few times it's not too bad, but I remember it being very annoying.
All of the unlockables are not particularly useful.

[h3]Review after completing it 100% with all achievements:[/h3]

I love this game with all my heart. it's easily in my top 5 games of all time. The characters, the music, the gameplay... everything about it is amazing.

I've done multiple playthroughs, speedruns, and randomizer runs, and I've enjoyed every single one of them. It honestly couldn't be more perfect.

I highly recommend it, even to people who have never played a metroidvania before.
The story, graphics, gameplay, and soundtrack in this game are all phenomenal. There's a reason Hollow Knight is a classic.
